refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation, task scheduling, and resource allocation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level; decomposing it into sub-FRs focused on key productivity drivers (equipment operation efficiency, task scheduling, resource allocation) allows targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations; verify that each sub-FR contributes to meeting or exceeding the productivity target.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operation efficiency to maximize output per hour.
FR2: Schedule tasks effectively to minimize idle time and maximize utilization.
FR3: Allocate resources dynamically to support continuous productivity.

candidate_dps: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms to improve operation efficiency.
DP2: Intelligent task scheduling system to optimize work sequences.
DP3: Dynamic resource allocation framework integrating telemetry and tasking data.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms to improve operation efficiency.

potential_sources: Interaction between vehicle control and task scheduling modules.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically decoupled from task scheduling and resource allocation but requires telemetry input; interfaces are well-defined.

assumptions_risks: Assumes telemetry data is accurate and timely.
Risk of over-dependence on vehicle control algorithms without sufficient integration with scheduling.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to address coupling and ensure holistic productivity optimization.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and software components responsible for vehicle operation and control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time vehicle and environment data for control algorithm input.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task assignments and scheduling information.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication with other modules and remote operators.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of vehicle control DP; low diffusion as control logic is centralized.
Broader scope; less focused containment for vehicle control DP.
Limited containment; control logic diffusion risk if implemented here.
Not relevant for vehicle control DP; high diffusion risk if misallocated.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Vehicle module best encapsulates the vehicle control DP with minimal diffusion and clear ownership.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.
Risk of duplicated control logic if interfaces are not well managed.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and ownership boundaries to mitigate diffusion risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (advanced vehicle control algorithms) logically satisfies the refined FR (optimize equipment operation efficiency) with clear verification paths.

diffusion_risks: Possible duplication of control logic if Teleoperation module also implements similar algorithms.
Interface complexity with telemetry and tasking data streams.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment and ownership suitability for the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data analysis and operational productivity metrics.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of vehicle control DP to Vehicle module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Further decompose remaining sub-FRs (task scheduling and resource allocation) with corresponding DP and IM assignments.
Establish interface protocols to prevent control logic duplication.
Plan integration testing across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution modules.

decision.justification: The current level decomposition and assignments are sound for the vehicle control aspect of productivity. Next step is to decompose and address the other sub-FRs (task scheduling and resource allocation) to complete the productivity KPI coverage.
next_level_sub_frs: FR2: Schedule tasks effectively to minimize idle time and maximize utilization.
FR3: Allocate resources dynamically to support continuous productivity.

revise.why: To refine and decompose the remaining productivity-related functional requirements for comprehensive coverage.

assumptions: Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
Vehicle control algorithms can be effectively encapsulated within the Vehicle module.
open_risks: Interface complexity and potential diffusion of control logic.
Integration challenges with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.
evidence_needed: Operational telemetry data demonstrating control algorithm effectiveness.
Verification results of productivity metrics during representative operations.
